title: meehania fargesii var. radicans
intro: Meehania fargesii var. radicans, also known as creeping Meehania, is a type of perennial flowering plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Meehania fargesii var. radicans typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the leaves and stems of the plant, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
